channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Does Iraq war hold lessons for Iran on regime change?
item.description: President Trump's war against Iran carries echoes of the 2003 war in Iraq. NPR's Leila Fadel explores the lessons learned to understand whether regime change stands a chance in neighboring Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's war against Iran carries echoes of the 2003 war in Iraq. NPR's Leila Fadel explores the lessons learned to understand whether regime change stands a chance in neighboring Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel needs three weeks to destroy Iran's military, Kurds say they're not guns for hire, Strait of Hormuz blockage risks global energy shock.
item.content:encoded: Israel needs three weeks to destroy Iran's military, Kurds say they're not guns for hire, Strait of Hormuz blockage risks global energy shock.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Energy analyst discusses how high Iran war could drive oil prices
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks energy industry analyst Clay Seigle how high oil prices could go amid the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks energy industry analyst Clay Seigle how high oil prices could go amid the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Kids' willpower is no match for fast food and screens. Try this instead
item.description: For decades, parents were told to help children build willpower like a muscle, to resist things like junk food and too much time on their screens. But new research suggests a better strategy.
item.content:encoded: For decades, parents were told to help children build willpower like a muscle, to resist things like junk food and too much time on their screens. But new research suggests a better strategy.
item.dc:creator: Michaeleen Doucleff

item.title: Afghans and Pakistanis living in Iran flee new conflict
item.description: Afghans and Pakistanis living in Iran flee US-Israeli strikes, making desperate journey through treacherous land borders.
item.content:encoded: Afghans and Pakistanis living in Iran flee US-Israeli strikes, making desperate journey through treacherous land borders.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Retired US Navy Vice Admiral discusses risk of lengthy Iran conflict
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with retired Navy Vice Admiral Robert Harward about the risk of the conflict with Iran turning into a long war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with retired Navy Vice Admiral Robert Harward about the risk of the conflict with Iran turning into a long war.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Israel needs weeks to destroy Iran's military, defense official says
item.description: A senior Israeli defense official tells NPR that Israel needs three more weeks to accomplish its goal of decimating Iran's military forces.
item.content:encoded: A senior Israeli defense official tells NPR that Israel needs three more weeks to accomplish its goal of decimating Iran's military forces.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Candace Owens takes on Erika Kirk in 'The Bride of Charlie'
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Slate staff writer Molly Olmstead about "The Bride of Charlie," a series by conservative pundit Candace Owens that takes on Erika Kirk, the widow of Charlie Kirk.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Slate staff writer Molly Olmstead about "The Bride of Charlie," a series by conservative pundit Candace Owens that takes on Erika Kirk, the widow of Charlie Kirk.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Can Europe still rely on the US to support international human rights?
item.description: Can Europe keep relying on the U.S. as a partner in supporting human rights around the world? Michel Martin asks the E.U.'s special representative for human rights Kajsa Ollongren.
item.content:encoded: Can Europe keep relying on the U.S. as a partner in supporting human rights around the world? Michel Martin asks the E.U.'s special representative for human rights Kajsa Ollongren.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Musical treasures collected by late Indianapolis Colts owner up for auction
item.description: The late Jim Irsay built one of the great collections of musical artifacts. His family is now auctioning it off, including instruments played by The Beatles, Nirvana, Eric Clapton and Bob Dylan. Nathalie Ferneau {NAT-ah-lee fer-NO} from Christie's previews the auction.
item.content:encoded: The late Jim Irsay built one of the great collections of musical artifacts. His family is now auctioning it off, including instruments played by The Beatles, Nirvana, Eric Clapton and Bob Dylan. Nathalie Ferneau {NAT-ah-lee fer-NO} from Christie's previews the auction.
